[2018-04-10 06:37] elyot: @willo We've discussed this before. It's kinda weird but maybe it just makes sense in this case. There's also corpus, asteri, and blood pact to think about. I'll bring it up at the next meeting.
[2018-04-10 06:41] elyot: @Redzone They already work, but there are some questions about how they should actually behave. Some people think they should be allowed to apply partial damage to them in a frontline manner, and some think they should only be able to frontline-click them if it results in a full kill. The latter is how it's currently implemented, but we were never sure if people were happy with it so we just never bothered releasing a unit with those two attributes
[2018-04-10 06:42] elyot: it's kinda an ugly bit of design inelegance regardless of which of the two ways it works, because in the former case, you get into a situation where adding attack requires you to undo a frontline click. And the latter case just pisses some people off because they think the game should let them do something but it doesn't.

[2018-04-10 12:20] elyot: @Esooa (Riley)#8714 @Plato So I investigated your casual match histories. Basically the code was doing what it was supposed to, but after running some simulations, I agree that it matches you against weak bots too often. I've adjusted the formulas.
[2018-04-10 12:21] elyot: here is the new code
[2018-04-10 12:21] elyot: `botDiff = self.casualBotRating[botID] - a['user_rating']
i = botDiff
newScore = random()*(0.4*abs(i)**1.5+abs(i)+150) + ((2 * (i-200)) if i > 200 else 0) + ((0.4 * (-200-i)) if i < -200 else 0)`
[2018-04-10 12:21] elyot: and then it takes min of that across all bots
[2018-04-10 12:24] elyot: I simulated some batches of 10k games against the current bots and now most of them will be against the strongest ones for you, it will be very rare to match the expert/adept ones and nearly impossible to match the weak/random ones
[2018-04-10 12:31] elyot: BottyMcBotFace is actually over 100 points stronger than Master Bot, it seems to be really strong against the field that it plays against
[2018-04-10 12:34] darkeisbein: what does BottyMcBotFace do?
[2018-04-10 12:35] elyot: Master Bot but has a huge opening book of strong rushes. It can either choose those, or play normal Master Bot builds, and it uses alpha-beta pruning to decide
[2018-04-10 12:37] darkeisbein: but what does BottyMcBotFace do differently?

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: @Esooa (Riley)#8714 @Plato So I investigated your casual match histories. Basically the code was doing what it was supposed to, but after running some simulations, I agree that it matches you against weak bots too often. I've adjusted the formulas.

messages.content: here is the new code

messages.author: elyot
messages.content:
`botDiff = self.casualBotRating[botID] - a['user_rating']
i = botDiff
newScore = random()*(0.4*abs(i)**1.5+abs(i)+150) + ((2 * (i-200)) if i > 200 else 0) + ((0.4 * (-200-i)) if i < -200 else 0)`

[2018-04-23 22:32] elyot: Elo sucks for new players, it doesn't converge fast enough. Elo with variable k-factor can have weird results with far apart matches and can be unstable in some cases (cause inflation or deflation)
[2018-04-23 22:32] elyot: the thing is, all rating systems converge to Elo at some point
[2018-04-23 22:32] elyot: it's mostly about what happens with new players that changes the system
[2018-04-23 22:38] elyot: In any case, we use a 2-parameter model with bayesian updates that estimates player performance using a logistic distribution

[2018-04-30 07:51] mrguy888: What is the deal with shalevV?

Things I know: Having a higher rating means your shalevV will be lower, lower shalevV means you gain and lose fewer points from the result of the game.

Things I want to know: It seems to lag behind a game. Is it because it changes based on your rating not on the result of the game? Does it lag exactly one game behind or is it more complicated? Is it only based on your rating or are there more factors to it? Does someone with 3000 shalevV move at exactly half the pace of someone at 6000 shalevV?

[2018-04-30 08:13] elyot: @Bemystic I think it's Steam's problem, we don't know why it happens to some people, but if anyone figures it out, we'd sure like to know. We're just terminating Prismata in the most normal way possible.
[2018-04-30 08:14] elyot: @SilentSlayers The updates are not related to the crashing, as far as I know. It might have just happened to update after a crash, because we do push small client updates from time to time.
[2018-04-30 08:14] elyot: The crashes can be for a variety of reasons, I can look into it if you give me a timestamp and a username
[2018-04-30 08:14] elyot: @mrguy888 shalevV is the variance parameter of a random distribution that represents a player's performance in a given prismata game, it always decreases until it hits a minimum
[2018-04-30 08:15] elyot: it's not like k-factor or anything like that, it's more like the sigma parameter in trueskill

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: @Bemystic I think it's Steam's problem, we don't know why it happens to some people, but if anyone figures it out, we'd sure like to know. We're just terminating Prismata in the most normal way possible.
